title: BICEPS, TRICEPS & FOREARMS
yesterday: Yesterday: Bootcamp — hammies, running, SkiErg, wall balls, Bulgarian split squats

exs.name: Contralateral Incline Face-Pulls
exs.cue: Lie on incline bench, pull cable/band across body to opposite ear, retract scapula at peak

exs.name: Kneeling Narrow-Grip Crunched Tricep Extensions
exs.cue: Kneel facing cable, elbows tucked tight, crunch torso slightly forward, full extension at bottom

exs.name: Forward-to-Lateral Rotating Curls – Supinated
exs.cue: Start curl forward (front plane), rotate arc laterally to finish — supinate fully at peak, control descent

exs.name: Behind-the-Back Tricep Pushdowns – Twisting Bilateral
exs.cue: Stand with cable at low anchor behind you, arms at sides — extend and twist through full lockout, keep elbows stable

exs.name: Leaning Hammer 21s with Rep Partials
exs.cue: Lean slightly forward, 7 bottom-half partials → 7 top-half partials → 7 full ROM; neutral grip throughout

exs.name: Rotating Lateral Tricep Extensions – Compound Variation
exs.cue: Arm extended laterally, rotate through pronated-to-neutral on the extension — targets lateral head, keep shoulder packed

exs.name: Bicep Peak Max-Extension Curls – Unilateral
exs.cue: Full stretch at bottom (shoulder slightly in front of elbow), drive through full supination at peak, squeeze hard at top

exs.name: Ground-Stop DB Skull-Crushers – Triple-Grip Combo
exs.target: 2-3 sets to failure (neutral → pronated → supinated, alternating)
exs.cue: Lower to floor with control, pause at ground-stop, press back — rotate grip each rep for triple-head stimulus

exs.name: Iso-Supination DB Side-Swings – Unilateral
exs.cue: Swing DB laterally from hip in an arc, supinate at peak — isometric hold 1s at top, slow eccentric return

exs.name: Pronated Reverse Preacher Curls – Narrow Grip
exs.cue: Forearms on pad, narrow overhand grip — full ROM from extension to peak flexion, brachioradialis focus

exs.name: High-Cable Concentration Wrist Flexion + AD-duction
exs.cue: Cable high, pull wrist into flexion then adduct (ulnar deviation) — isolate forearm flexors and FCU, controlled tempo
